name: Cellpose_segment_image
description: Segment cells or nuclei in a single microscopy image LOCALLY using the deep-learning Cellpose model (no API, no key). Given a local image file path, returns the number of segmented objects, per-object area (pixels) and centroid (y,x), the image shape, and optionally the path to a saved integer label-mask image. Choose 'model_type' = 'cyto3'/'cyto'/'cyto2' for whole-cell/cytoplasm segmentation or 'nuclei' for nuclei. NOTE: cellpose 3.x honors 'model_type'; cellpose 4.x uses a single unified model (CPSAM) and ignores 'model_type' (the 'model_used' field and a 'note' report what actually ran). Requires the optional 'cellpose' package (pip install cellpose; pulls in torch). On first use cellpose downloads model weights (small in 3.x, ~1 GB CPSAM in 4.x) and caches them, so the first call is slow; later calls reuse cached weights. Returns a clean error if cellpose is not installed or the image cannot be read.

image_path.description: Path to a local microscopy image file (.tif, .tiff, .png, .jpg, .jpeg, .bmp).

model_type.description: Cellpose model to use. 'cyto3'/'cyto'/'cyto2' for cells/cytoplasm, 'nuclei' for nuclei. Honored on cellpose 3.x; ignored by the unified model on 4.x. Default 'cyto3'.

diameter.description: Expected object diameter in pixels. Omit or 0 to let cellpose estimate it automatically.

channels.description: Two-element [cytoplasm, nucleus] channel spec. Use [0,0] for a grayscale image (default). Used by cellpose 3.x; 4.x infers channels.

save_mask.description: If true, save the integer label mask as a 16-bit PNG and return its path. Default false.

mask_output_path.description: Where to write the mask when save_mask is true. Defaults to '<image_path>_cp_masks.png'.

data.description: Successful segmentation. Mirrors the content of the 'data' field returned by the tool.

image_path.description: The input image path that was segmented.

model_type_requested.description: The model_type the caller asked for.

model_used.description: Identifier of the cellpose model that actually produced the segmentation.

num_objects.description: Number of segmented cells/nuclei (distinct nonzero labels in the mask).

image_shape.description: Shape of the output label mask, e.g. [height, width].

label.description: Integer label of this object in the mask.

mask_path.description: Path to the saved label-mask image, or null if save_mask was false.

note.description: Present when the cellpose build ignored model_type (unified-model builds).

mask_save_error.description: Present only if saving the mask failed (segmentation still succeeded).

oneOf.description: Error response (e.g. cellpose not installed, missing/unreadable image, segmentation failure).
